twitter.content:
RT by @bcherny: Three years ago today.
Three years ago today.

R to @bcherny: Quick thank you to everyone who's been building with Claude Code, both the early crowd and everyone who showed up this year. It’s only thanks to your feedback that we can make the product a little better every day.
Quick thank you to everyone who's been building with Claude Code, both the early crowd and everyone who showed up this year. It’s only thanks to your feedback that we can make the product a little better every day.

We doubled Claude usage on weekends, and outside 5–11am PT on weekdays for the next 2 weeks.
We doubled Claude usage on weekends, and outside 5–11am PT on weekdays for the next 2 weeks.


Claude (@claudeai)

A small thank you to everyone using Claude: We’re doubling usage outside our peak hours for the next two weeks.

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/claudeai/status/2032911276226257206#m

RT by @bcherny: Starting today, Claude no longer defaults to text. Claude is learning to choose the best medium for each response — based on the task, the data, and what's most useful for the person. Give it a try!
Starting today, Claude no longer defaults to text. Claude is learning to choose the best medium for each response — based on the task, the data, and what's most useful for the person. Give it a try!


Claude (@claudeai)

Claude can now build interactive charts and diagrams, directly i

RT by @bcherny: A few end of week ships:

You can now set effort to 'max' which reasons for longer and uses as many tokens as needed. This will spend your usage limits more quickly so you have to activate it per session. 

Hit /effort to try it.
A few end of week ships:

You can now set effort to 'max' which reasons for longer and uses as many tokens as needed. This will spend your usage limits more quickly so you have to activate it per session. 

Hit /effort to try it.

Video

🤯 You can now launch Claude Code sessions on your laptop *from your phone*

This blew my mind the first time I tried it
🤯 You can now launch Claude Code sessions on your laptop *from your phone*

This blew my mind the first time I tried it


Noah Zweben (@noahzweben)

Remote Control - Session Spawning:
Run claude remote-control and then spawn a NEW local session in the mobile app.

* Out to Max, Team, and Enterprise (>=2.1.74)
*Have GH set up on mobile (relaxing soon)
* Working on speeding up

R to @bcherny: Personally, I've been using exclusively 1M context for the last few months and loving it.

You can also customize your auto-compact threshold with the CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW env var.

More here: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config
Personally, I've been using exclusively 1M context for the last few months and loving it.

You can also customize your auto-compact threshold with the CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW env var.

More here: code.claude.com/docs/en/mode…

🎁 Happy Friday - Opus 4.6 1M is now the default Opus model for Claude Code users on Max, Team, and Enterprise plans.

Pro and Sonnet users can opt in with /extra-usage.
🎁 Happy Friday - Opus 4.6 1M is now the default Opus model for Claude Code users on Max, Team, and Enterprise plans.

Pro and Sonnet users can opt in with /extra-usage.


Claude (@claudeai)

1 million context window: Now generally available for Claude Opus 4.6 and Claude Sonnet 4.6.





— https://nitter.net/claudeai/status/20325

Update: this is now rolled out to 100% of users
Update: this is now rolled out to 100% of users


Boris Cherny (@bcherny)

🎶 I've been using voice mode to write much of my CLI code this last week

Can't wait to hear what you think.



— https://nitter.net/bcherny/status/2028629573722939789#m

RT by @bcherny: Claude can now build interactive charts and diagrams, directly in the chat.

Available today in beta on all plans, including free.

Try it out: http://claude.ai
Claude can now build interactive charts and diagrams, directly in the chat.

Available today in beta on all plans, including free.

Try it out: claude.ai

Video

RT by @bcherny: I've seen enough. Anthropic is the new default for businesses.

Says latest Ramp AI Index.
I've seen enough. Anthropic is the new default for businesses.

Says latest Ramp AI Index.

RT by @bcherny: Claude for Excel and Claude for PowerPoint now sync together seamlessly.

When you’ve got more than one file open, Claude shares the full context of your conversation between them.

Pull data from spreadsheets, build out tables, and update a deck — without re-explaining a step.
Claude for Excel and Claude for PowerPoint now sync together seamlessly.

When you’ve got more than one file open, Claude shares the full context of your conversation between them.

Pull data from spreadsh

btw
btw


Thariq (@trq212)

We just added /btw to Claude Code! 

Use it to have side chain conversations while Claude is working.

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/trq212/status/2031506296697131352#m

New in Claude Code: Code Review. A team of agents runs a deep review on every PR.

We built it for ourselves first. Code output per Anthropic engineer is up 200% this year and reviews were the bottleneck

Personally, I’ve been using it for a few weeks and have found it catches many real bugs that I would not have noticed otherwise
New in Claude Code: Code Review. A team of agents runs a deep review on every PR.

We built it for ourselves first. Code output per Anthropic engineer is up 200% this

R to @bcherny: Learn more: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/scheduled-tasks
Learn more: code.claude.com/docs/en/sche…

Released today: /loop

/loop is a powerful new way to schedule recurring tasks, for up to 3 days at a time

eg. “/loop babysit all my PRs. Auto-fix build issues and when comments come in, use a worktree agent to fix them”

eg. “/loop every morning use the Slack MCP to give me a summary of top posts I was tagged in”

Let us know what you think!

RT by @bcherny: Want to host Claude meetups in your city? We'll cover the funding, send swag, and give you monthly API credits for your demos. 

You also get access to pre-release features and a private slack with the team! Go apply 💛

RT by @bcherny: Today we're launching local scheduled tasks in Claude Code desktop. 

Create a schedule for tasks that you want to run regularly. They'll run as long as your computer is awake.
